{"credits": [{"type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/73954294?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/smakarim/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/smakarim/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/smakarim/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/smakarim/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/smakarim", "id": 73954294, "login": "smakarim", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjczOTU0Mjk0", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/smakarim/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/smakarim/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/smakarim/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/smakarim/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/smakarim/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/smakarim", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-55792", "cvss": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 6.0, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:P/PR:L/UI:N/VC:H/VI:N/VA:N/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-200", "name": "Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor"}], "description": "The `dataUrl()` Twig function is included in Craft’s Twig sandbox allowlist, allowing any control panel user granted the `utility:system-messages` permission to embed a file-reading payload into system email templates. When those emails are sent, the server reads the target file and returns its contents as a base64-encoded data URL embedded in the email body. The .env file, which typically contains the database password, CRAFT_SECURITY_KEY, and third-party API keys, passes all of Craft’s existing `dataUrl()` protection checks and is fully exfiltrated. Obtaining CRAFT_SECURITY_KEY enables an attacker to forge session tokens and escalate to full admin account takeover.

## Details
Affected versions: Craft CMS 4.x, 5.x (confirmed against 5.9.19)

The vulnerability arises from the combination of three code facts:
1. dataUrl is in the Twig sandbox allowlist
src/config/twig-sandbox.php, line 115:
php'allowedFunctions' => [
    ...
    'dataUrl',   // ← allows file reading inside sandboxed templates
    ...
],

2. Html::dataUrl() does not block dotfiles
src/helpers/Html.php, lines 1065–1090. The function applies four checks before reading a file:

Must be within the project root .env is at the root
Must not be in a system directory (config/, vendor/, storage/, templates/) .env is not
Must not be a .php file  .env has no extension
File must exist .env always exists in a Craft install

There is no check for dotfiles or specifically for .env. All four checks pass silently and file_get_contents() is called, with the result returned as data:text/plain;base64,....

3. System message body is rendered via renderSandboxedString()
src/mail/Mailer.php, lines 181–183:
php$subject = $view->renderSandboxedString($systemMessage->subject, $variables);
$textBody = $view->renderSandboxedString($systemMessage->body, $variables);
$htmlBody = $view->renderSandboxedString($systemMessage->body, $variables, escapeHtml: true);

Any body content saved to a system message is executed inside the Twig sandbox when the email renders. Because dataUrl is in allowedFunctions, the sandbox policy permits its execution without restriction.

Access control: The utility:system-messages permission is a non-admin CP permission grantable to any user group via Settings > Users > Groups. It is not restricted to admins.

## Impact
Vulnerability type: Sensitive File Disclosure / Server-Side File Read
Who is impacted: Any Craft CMS 4.x or 5.x installation where at least one non-admin control panel user has been granted the utility:system-messages permission, and email sending is configured.

Resources:

- https://github.com/craftcms/cms/pull/18559", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00268, "percentile": 0.18502}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-287w-mxq6-x2cp", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-07-06T21:29:49Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-287w-mxq6-x2cp", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-287w-mxq6-x2cp"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-55792"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-07-02T00:16:44Z", "published_at": "2026-07-06T21:29:49Z", "references": ["https://github.com/craftcms/cms/security/advisories/GHSA-287w-mxq6-x2cp", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-55792", "https://github.com/craftcms/cms/pull/18559", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-287w-mxq6-x2cp"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/craftcms/cms/security-advisories/GHSA-287w-mxq6-x2cp", "severity": "medium", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/craftcms/cms", "summary": "Craft CMS: Sensitive File Disclosure / Server-Side File Read", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-07-06T21:29:50Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-287w-mxq6-x2cp", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "4.18.0", "package": {"ecosystem": "composer", "name": "craftcms/cms"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 4.0.0-RC1, < 4.18.0"}, {"first_patched_version": "5.10.0", "package": {"ecosystem": "composer", "name": "craftcms/cms"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 5.0.0-RC1, < 5.10.0"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: Craft CMS: Sensitive File Disclosure / Server-Side File Read
